description.value:
Your formal speeches can move courts, councils, guilds, and other structured audiences. When you use Society instead of Performance to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EEDElIyin4z60PXx] with Public Speaker before a formal audience, you can also attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.OX4fy22hQgUHDr0q] on that audience using Society. This applies only to listeners who understand the social, civic, academic, legal, noble, or institutional context of your speech. Instead of targeting a single creature, you can target a small formal audience, such as a noble household, council chamber, guild meeting, courtly reception, academic panel, or diplomatic gathering. The GM determines the DC, usually using the highest relevant Will DC or a standard DC for the audience’s level.

On a success, the audience’s general attitude toward your position improves by one step for the purpose of the current topic or proceeding. On a critical success, you also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to one @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.iCv8KTZ5PcF4GqeV] attempt related to that audience or institution before the end of the day. This doesn’t force agreement, grant a favor, replace a Request, or affect creatures who reject the relevant social context.
